dimensions.note: Workflow-scope report. It consumes @jentz/aws-rds-reservations rows already persisted in swamp and makes no AWS calls of its own, so it handles no credentials or secrets.

info on run start; warn on every degraded path (thrown failure, missing collector step, no-regions) and on per-artifact read/decode/schema skips, each naming spec/handle. All logging goes through tryLog, which tolerates a missing logger; structured placeholders throughout.

Single coherent degraded path: any thrown computation, a missing collector step, or a no-regions sweep funnels through degrade(), which resets every derived source together so a degraded payload is unambiguously empty (never a misleading '0 to buy, fully covered'). Per-artifact read/decode/schema failures skip just that handle. The report itself never throws.

93 Deno.test cases across tests/ (collect+render and the main aggregation suite) plus a colocated sanity test. Covers degraded paths (thrown, no matching step, no_regions), per-artifact skip on read/decode/schema failure, size-flex netting, non-size-flex carve-outs, and per-account aggregation.

Pure read-and-render with no persisted resources and no external mutation; re-running over the same upstream data yields the same report. Resilient to partial/corrupt upstream artifacts (skips them) and to degraded upstream sweeps (degraded=true).

Makes no external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only 'contract' is the @jentz/aws-rds-reservations resource schemas, which it re-validates with Zod safeParse per row (InstanceRecordSchema/ReservedRecordSchema/ScanErrorSchema) before use.
Creates no cloud resources, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes via context.dataRepository.getContent and returns markdown + JSON; nothing to clean up or track.
README examples and rendered output use placeholder workflow names, regions, and synthetic account labels. No real IPs, internal hostnames, account ids, or topology in the published surface.
